annotations.label: General topology

anchor.snippet: is the branch of topology that deals with the basic set-theoretic definitions

annotations.note: A branch of mathematics, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Topology on a set

anchor.snippet: is called a topology on X if

annotations.note: The TopologicalSpace class bundles the set of open sets with the topology axioms.

annotations.label: Topological space

anchor.snippet: is called a topological space

annotations.note: A topological space is a type with a TopologicalSpace instance.

annotations.label: Open and closed sets

anchor.snippet: are called open sets in X

annotations.note: IsOpen (and dually IsClosed) are the membership predicates for the topology.

annotations.label: Base (basis) for a topology

anchor.snippet: A base (or basis ) B for a topological space X with topology T is a collection of open sets

annotations.note: IsTopologicalBasis expresses that a collection of open sets generates the topology.

annotations.label: Subspace topology

anchor.snippet: can be given the subspace topology in which the open sets are the intersections

annotations.note: The subtype instance is the topology induced by the inclusion map.

annotations.label: Product topology

anchor.snippet: the product can be given the product topology

annotations.note: The product topology on a binary product is the inf of the two induced topologies.

annotations.label: Quotient topology

anchor.snippet: the quotient topology on Y is the collection of subsets of Y that have open inverse images under f

annotations.note: The quotient/coinduced topology declares a set open iff its preimage is open.

annotations.label: Discrete topology

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the discrete topology , in which every subset is open

annotations.note: DiscreteTopology asserts the topology equals the bottom (every set open).

annotations.label: Trivial (indiscrete) topology

anchor.snippet: any set can be given the trivial topology

annotations.note: IndiscreteTopology asserts the topology equals the top (only ∅ and univ open).

annotations.label: Cofinite topology

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the cofinite topology

annotations.note: CofiniteTopology is a type synonym carrying the topology whose open sets are cofinite sets and ∅.

annotations.label: Cocountable topology

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the cocountable topology

annotations.note: No cocountable-topology construction was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standard topology on R

anchor.section: Topologies on the real and complex numbers
anchor.snippet: The standard topology on R is generated by the open intervals

annotations.note: ℝ carries its standard topology via the (pseudo)metric/order topology instance.

annotations.label: Lower limit topology

anchor.section: Topologies on the real and complex numbers
anchor.snippet: The real line can also be given the lower limit topology

annotations.note: The Sorgenfrey/lower-limit line exists only in Counterexamples, not within Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Metric topology

anchor.snippet: Every metric space can be given a metric topology

annotations.note: PseudoMetricSpace extends UniformSpace/TopologicalSpace, equipping every metric space with its metric topology.

annotations.label: Finite topological spaces

anchor.snippet: Such spaces are called finite topological spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has Finite and TopologicalSpace but no dedicated finite-topological-space theory.

annotations.label: Natural topology on a manifold

anchor.snippet: Every manifold has a natural topology , since it is locally Euclidean

annotations.note: Manifolds are defined as ChartedSpaces requiring a topology as input, rather than deriving it from being locally Euclidean.

annotations.label: Zariski topology

anchor.snippet: The Zariski topology is defined algebraically on the spectrum of a ring

annotations.note: zariskiTopology is the topology instance on the prime spectrum of a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Sierpiński space

anchor.snippet: The Sierpiński space is the simplest non-discrete topological space

annotations.note: sierpinskiSpace is the topology on Prop generated by {True}.

annotations.label: Order topology on an ordinal

anchor.snippet: may be endowed with the order topology generated by the intervals

annotations.note: Ordinals carry the order topology via a Preorder.topology + OrderTopology instance (Mathlib.SetTheory.Ordinal.Topology).

annotations.label: Continuity (neighborhood)

anchor.snippet: Continuity is expressed in terms of neighborhoods

annotations.note: ContinuousAt is defined as Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x)), i.e. via neighborhoods.

annotations.label: Functions out of a discrete space are continuous

anchor.snippet: if a set X is given the discrete topology , all functions

annotations.note: Any function from a discrete-topology space is continuous.

annotations.label: Functions out of an indiscrete space

anchor.snippet: the only continuous functions are the constant functions

annotations.note: No lemma stating continuous maps out of an indiscrete space are exactly the constant functions was found.

annotations.label: Neighborhood definition of continuity

anchor.snippet: The following criterion expresses continuity in terms of neighborhoods

annotations.note: continuousAt_def characterizes continuity at x by preimages of neighborhoods being neighborhoods.

annotations.label: Continuity via limit (Hausdorff target)

anchor.snippet: if the target space is Hausdorff

annotations.note: No specific 'continuity equals limit-preservation given a Hausdorff target' lemma was found.

annotations.label: Continuity at isolated points

anchor.snippet: At an isolated point , every function is continuous

annotations.note: No lemma asserting every function is continuous at an isolated point was found.

annotations.label: Continuity and limits

anchor.snippet: A function is continuous only if it takes limits of sequences to limits of sequences

annotations.note: Continuous.tendsto shows a continuous function preserves filter limits at every point.

annotations.label: Sequentially continuous function

anchor.snippet: is sequentially continuous if whenever a sequence

annotations.note: SeqContinuous is defined by preservation of convergent sequences.

annotations.label: Continuous implies sequentially continuous

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function is sequentially continuous

annotations.note: Continuous.seqContinuous gives that continuity implies sequential continuity.

annotations.label: Converse for first-countable spaces

anchor.snippet: If X is a first-countable space and countable choice holds, then the converse also holds

annotations.note: continuous_iff_seqContinuous holds in any SequentialSpace, which first-countable spaces are.

annotations.label: Continuity characterized by nets

anchor.snippet: Continuous functions preserve limits of nets, and in fact this property characterizes continuous functions

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes continuity via filter convergence (Tendsto), which subsumes nets.

annotations.label: Closure and interior operators

anchor.snippet: the topology can also be determined by a closure operator

annotations.note: closure and interior are defined as the standard topological operators.

annotations.label: Continuity via closure operator

anchor.snippet: is continuous in the sense above if and only if for all subsets A of X

annotations.note: This lemma states f is continuous iff f(closure A) ⊆ closure(f A) for all A.

annotations.label: Composition of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: then so is the composition

annotations.note: Continuous.comp gives continuity of the composition of continuous functions.

annotations.label: Continuous image of compact space

anchor.snippet: then f ( X ) is compact

annotations.note: IsCompact.image shows the continuous image of a compact set is compact.

annotations.label: Continuous image of connected space

anchor.snippet: then f ( X ) is connected

annotations.note: IsConnected.image (and IsPreconnected.image) give continuous images of connected sets are connected.

annotations.label: Continuous image of path-connected space

anchor.snippet: then f ( X ) is path-connected

annotations.note: IsPathConnected.image gives the continuous image of a path-connected set is path-connected.

annotations.label: Continuous image of Lindelöf space

anchor.snippet: then f ( X ) is Lindelöf

annotations.note: IsLindelof.image gives the continuous image of a Lindelöf set is Lindelöf.

annotations.label: Continuous image of separable space

anchor.snippet: then f ( X ) is separable

annotations.note: The continuous image of a separable set is separable, verified for (pseudo)metrizable spaces.

annotations.label: Coarser and finer topologies

anchor.snippet: is said to be coarser than another topology

annotations.note: The partial order ≤ on TopologicalSpace encodes the coarser/finer relation.

annotations.label: Identity map and comparison of topologies

anchor.snippet: see also comparison of topologies

annotations.note: continuous_id_iff_le states the identity is continuous iff the domain topology is finer.

annotations.label: Open map

anchor.snippet: is an open map , for which images of open sets are open

annotations.note: IsOpenMap is the predicate that images of open sets are open.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bijective continuous function with continuous inverse function is called a homeomorphism

annotations.note: Homeomorph is a bijection continuous in both directions.

annotations.label: Continuous bijection compact to Hausdorff is homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: If a continuous bijection has as its domain a compact space

annotations.note: A continuous bijection from a compact space to a T2 space is a homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Final topology

anchor.snippet: the final topology on S is defined by letting the open sets of S

annotations.note: coinduced gives the finest topology making a map continuous; family final topologies are sup of coinduced.

annotations.label: Initial topology

anchor.snippet: the initial topology on S has a basis of open sets

annotations.note: induced gives the coarsest topology making a map continuous; family initial topologies are inf of induced.

annotations.label: Topology determined by continuous functions

anchor.snippet: A topology on a set S is uniquely determined by the class of all continuous functions

annotations.note: No uniqueness theorem framed as 'topology determined by the class of all continuous functions' was found.

annotations.label: Compact space

anchor.snippet: is called compact if each of its open covers has a finite subcover

annotations.note: CompactSpace/IsCompact capture the finite-subcover property (via filters).

annotations.label: Quasi-compact (Bourbaki terminology)

anchor.snippet: use the term quasi-compact for the general notion

annotations.note: Mathlib's IsCompact does not require Hausdorff, matching the Bourbaki quasi-compact notion.

annotations.label: Closed intervals in R are compact

anchor.snippet: Every closed interval in R of finite length is compact

annotations.note: isCompact_Icc proves Icc compact for any conditionally complete linear order with order topology, ℝ included.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem

anchor.snippet: a set is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded

annotations.note: Heine–Borel is proved for proper metric spaces (subsuming ℝⁿ): compact iff closed and bounded.

annotations.label: Continuous image of compact is compact

anchor.snippet: Every continuous image of a compact space is compact

annotations.note: IsCompact.image gives the continuous image of a compact set is compact.

annotations.label: Compact subset of Hausdorff is closed

anchor.snippet: A compact subset of a Hausdorff space is closed

annotations.note: IsCompact.isClosed shows compact subsets of a T2 space are closed.

annotations.label: Continuous bijection compact to Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: Every continuous bijection from a compact space to a Hausdorff space is necessarily a homeomorphism

annotations.note: A continuous bijection from a compact space to a T2 space is a homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Sequential compactness of compact metric spaces

anchor.snippet: Every sequence of points in a compact metric space has a convergent subsequence

annotations.note: IsCompact.tendsto_subseq extracts a convergent subsequence from any sequence in a compact set (metric is a special case).

annotations.label: Embedding of compact manifolds

anchor.snippet: Every compact finite-dimensional manifold can be embedded in some Euclidean space

annotations.note: The weak Whitney embedding theorem embeds compact manifolds into some EuclideanSpace.

annotations.label: Connected and disconnected spaces

anchor.snippet: is said to be disconnected if it is the union of two disjoint nonempty open sets

annotations.note: ConnectedSpace/IsPreconnected capture connectedness; disconnection is its negation rather than a named predicate.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for connectedness

anchor.snippet: the following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: Several equivalent characterizations exist (e.g. via disjoint opens, clopen sets in preconnectedSpace_iff_clopen).

annotations.label: Intervals in R are connected

anchor.snippet: Every interval in R is connected

annotations.note: Intervals are preconnected in any densely ordered space with order topology, ℝ included.

annotations.label: Continuous image of connected space

anchor.snippet: The continuous image of a connected space is connected

annotations.note: IsPreconnected.image gives the continuous image of a connected set is connected.

annotations.label: Connected components

anchor.snippet: are called the connected components of the space

annotations.note: connectedComponent x is the union of all preconnected sets containing x.

annotations.label: Components form a partition

anchor.snippet: form a partition of X

annotations.note: connectedComponentSetoid makes the components equivalence classes, hence a partition of X.

annotations.label: Quasi-component equals component under conditions

anchor.snippet: the equality holds if X is compact Hausdorff or locally connected

annotations.note: Quasi-components are not defined in Mathlib, so this equality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Totally disconnected space

anchor.snippet: is called totally disconnected

annotations.note: TotallyDisconnectedSpace requires all connected components to be singletons.

annotations.label: Totally separated space

anchor.snippet: a space X is called totally separated if

annotations.note: TotallySeparatedSpace requires any two points to be separated by a clopen partition.

annotations.label: Totally separated implies totally disconnected

anchor.snippet: Clearly any totally separated space is totally disconnected

annotations.note: This instance derives total disconnectedness from total separatedness.

annotations.label: Path

anchor.snippet: A path from a point x to a point y in a topological space X is a continuous function

annotations.note: Path is the bundled continuous map from [0,1] with given endpoints.

annotations.label: Path-component

anchor.snippet: A path-component of X is an equivalence class

annotations.note: pathComponent x is the set of points joined to x by a path.

annotations.label: Path-connected space

anchor.snippet: The space X is said to be path-connected

annotations.note: PathConnectedSpace requires nonemptiness and that any two points be joined by a path.

annotations.label: Path-connected implies connected

anchor.snippet: Every path-connected space is connected

annotations.note: This instance shows path-connected spaces are connected (also IsPathConnected.isConnected).

annotations.label: Connected but not path-connected spaces

anchor.snippet: examples of connected spaces that are not path-connected include the extended long line

annotations.note: The long line is absent and the topologist's sine curve lives in Counterexamples, outside Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Connected iff path-connected for subsets of R

anchor.snippet: subsets of the real line R are connected if and only if they are path-connected

annotations.note: No equivalence of connectedness and path-connectedness specialized to subsets of ℝ was found.

annotations.label: Connectedness equals path-connectedness for finite spaces

anchor.snippet: connectedness and path-connectedness are the same for finite topological spaces

annotations.note: No result equating connectedness and path-connectedness for finite spaces was found.

annotations.label: Product topology

anchor.snippet: the product topology on X is defined as the coarsest topology

annotations.note: Pi.topologicalSpace is the inf of the topologies induced by the projections, i.e. the coarsest making them continuous.

annotations.label: Basis for finite products

anchor.snippet: the products of base elements of the X i gives a basis for the product

annotations.note: IsTopologicalBasis.prod shows products of basis elements form a basis (binary; isTopologicalBasis_pi for the indexed case).

annotations.label: Subbase for product topology

anchor.snippet: form a subbase for the topology on X

annotations.note: The product topology is defined as inf of induced topologies (the subbase content) but no explicit subbase lemma was found.

annotations.label: Box topology

anchor.snippet: forms a basis for what is called the box topology on X

annotations.note: The box topology is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Box topology finer than product topology

anchor.snippet: the box topology is finer than the product topology

annotations.note: Without a box topology, this comparison is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem

anchor.snippet: the (arbitrary) product of compact spaces is compact

annotations.note: isCompact_pi_infinite (and instance Pi.compactSpace) give Tychonoff's theorem for arbitrary products.

annotations.label: T0 (Kolmogorov) space

anchor.snippet: X is T 0 , or Kolmogorov

annotations.note: T0Space states topologically indistinguishable points are equal.

annotations.label: T1 (Fréchet) space

anchor.snippet: X is T 1 , or accessible or Fréchet

annotations.note: T1Space states every singleton is closed.

annotations.label: Hausdorff (T2) space

anchor.snippet: X is Hausdorff , or T 2 or separated

annotations.note: T2Space separates distinct points by disjoint open neighborhoods.

annotations.label: Urysohn (T2½) space

anchor.snippet: X is T 2½ , or Urysohn

annotations.note: T25Space separates distinct points by neighborhoods with disjoint closures.

annotations.label: Regular (T3) space

anchor.snippet: X is regular , or T 3

annotations.note: RegularSpace separates points from closed sets; T3Space adds T0 (regular form is exactly captured).

annotations.label: Tychonoff (T3½) space

anchor.snippet: X is Tychonoff , or T 3½

annotations.note: CompletelyRegularSpace separates points from closed sets via continuous functions; T35Space adds T0.

annotations.label: Normal (T4) space

anchor.snippet: X is normal , or T 4

annotations.note: NormalSpace separates disjoint closed sets by disjoint open sets; T4Space adds T1.

annotations.label: Urysohn's lemma

anchor.snippet: this is Urysohn's lemma

annotations.note: This is Urysohn's lemma: disjoint closed sets in a normal space are separated by a [0,1]-valued continuous function.

annotations.label: Completely normal (T5) space

anchor.snippet: X is completely normal , or T 5 or completely T 4

annotations.note: CompletelyNormalSpace separates separated sets; T5Space adds T1.

annotations.label: Perfectly normal (T6) space

anchor.snippet: X is perfectly normal , or T 6 or perfectly T 4

annotations.note: PerfectlyNormalSpace is normal with closed sets being Gδ; T6Space adds T0.

annotations.label: Tietze extension theorem

anchor.snippet: The Tietze extension theorem

annotations.note: This is the Tietze extension theorem extending continuous functions off a closed set in a normal space.

annotations.label: Axiom of countability

anchor.snippet: An axiom of countability is a property of certain mathematical objects

annotations.note: This is a meta-classification of properties, not itself a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Sequential space

anchor.snippet: a set is open if every sequence convergent to a point in the set is eventually in the set

annotations.note: SequentialSpace requires sequentially closed sets to be closed.

annotations.label: First-countable space

anchor.snippet: every point has a countable neighbourhood basis

annotations.note: FirstCountableTopology requires each neighborhood filter to be countably generated.

annotations.label: Second-countable space

anchor.snippet: the topology has a countable base

annotations.note: SecondCountableTopology requires a countable topological basis.

annotations.label: Separable space

anchor.snippet: there exists a countable dense subspace

annotations.note: SeparableSpace requires a countable dense subset.

annotations.label: Lindelöf space

anchor.snippet: every open cover has a countable subcover

annotations.note: LindelofSpace requires every open cover to have a countable subcover.

annotations.label: σ-compact space

anchor.snippet: there exists a countable cover by compact spaces

annotations.note: SigmaCompactSpace requires the space to be a countable union of compact sets.

annotations.label: First-countable implies sequential

anchor.snippet: Every first countable space is sequential

annotations.note: First-countable spaces are Fréchet–Urysohn, hence sequential.

annotations.label: Second-countable implications

anchor.snippet: Every second-countable space is first-countable, separable, and Lindelöf

annotations.note: Instances give second-countable ⇒ first-countable, ⇒ separable (to_separableSpace), and ⇒ Lindelöf.

annotations.label: σ-compact implies Lindelöf

anchor.snippet: Every σ-compact space is Lindelöf

annotations.note: IsSigmaCompact.isLindelof shows σ-compact sets are Lindelöf.

annotations.label: Metric space is first-countable

anchor.snippet: A metric space is first-countable

annotations.note: Any uniform space with countably generated uniformity (so any metric space) is first-countable.

annotations.label: Equivalences for metric spaces

anchor.snippet: For metric spaces second-countability, separability, and the Lindelöf property are all equivalent

annotations.note: The key direction separable ⇒ second-countable for metric spaces is formalized, but the triple equivalence is not bundled in one decl.

annotations.label: Metric space

anchor.snippet: is an ordered pair

annotations.note: MetricSpace bundles a distance function with the metric axioms.

annotations.label: Metric spaces are paracompact, Hausdorff, normal

anchor.snippet: is paracompact and Hausdorff , and thus normal

annotations.note: Metric spaces are paracompact and T2, hence normal (T4Space.of_paracompactSpace_t2Space).

annotations.label: Metrization theorems

anchor.snippet: The metrization theorems provide necessary and sufficient conditions

annotations.note: Urysohn's metrization theorem (T3 + second-countable ⇒ metrizable) is formalized.

annotations.label: Baire category theorem

anchor.snippet: The Baire category theorem says

annotations.note: In a BaireSpace a countable intersection of dense open sets is dense (complete metric spaces are BaireSpaces).

annotations.label: Open subspace of a Baire space

anchor.snippet: Any open subspace of a Baire space is itself a Baire space

annotations.note: IsOpen.baireSpace shows open subspaces of a Baire space are Baire.

annotations.label: Continuum

anchor.snippet: A continuum (pl continua ) is a nonempty compact connected metric space

annotations.note: No topological 'continuum' (compact connected metric space) definition exists; Cardinal.continuum is unrelated.

annotations.label: Topological algebra

anchor.snippet: A topological algebra A over a topological field K is a topological vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib models topological algebras via Algebra plus ContinuousSMul rather than a bundled TopologicalAlgebra class.

annotations.label: Unital associative topological algebra is a topological ring

anchor.snippet: A unital associative topological algebra is a topological ring

annotations.note: No theorem deriving a topological ring structure from a unital associative topological algebra was found.

annotations.label: Metrizable space

anchor.snippet: a metrizable space is a topological space that is homeomorphic to a metric space

annotations.note: MetrizableSpace asserts the topology comes from a metric (PseudoMetrizableSpace for the pseudometric case).
